verse_specific_anchors: avyakta-ādīni / vyakta-madhyāni / avyakta-nidhanāni — the three-phase compound
bhūtāni (beings) as the subject — phenomenal beings, not the imperishable Self
tatra kā paridevanā (what lamentation is there?) — the rhetorical conclusion specific to 2.28
the perceptual-asymmetry argument distinct from concessive (2.26) or paired-cycle (2.27)
verdict_reason: The engineering analog anchors specifically on the three-phase compound structure. The framing 'pre-existence as need / manifest implementation / post-existence as residual spec' directly mirrors avyakta-ādi / vyakta-madhya / avyakta-nidhana. This would NOT apply to 2.26 (which is binary concessive, not three-phase), nor to 2.27 (which is paired symmetric, not bracketed-middle), nor to 2.20 (which is unitary-imperishable). The three-phase structure is the verse-specific signal.
